paper4.title: Critical Thinking
paper4.taskInstructions: Use the paper to find the correct answers to the questions on the right. You may submit as many times as needed — your time and number of attempts are recorded. All multiple-choice questions must be answered correctly before you can proceed.

questions.text: The study found that knowledge workers self-reported enacting critical thinking in approximately what proportion of their GenAI task examples?
The paper states two explicit research questions. What are they, and how do they differ from each other?
Which of the following was NOT identified as a key motivator for critical thinking among knowledge workers using GenAI?
The paper describes a shift in cognitive effort for Analysis, Synthesis, and Evaluation. What specific shift did the authors observe, and what new role does this create for knowledge workers?
According to the regression analysis, which factor had the strongest negative association with perceived enaction of critical thinking?

options.A: Low tendency to reflect on work
options.B: High confidence in GenAI doing the task

questions.text: How were participants recruited for the study, and what was the minimum usage requirement to qualify?

options.A: LinkedIn outreach; used GenAI at least once ever
options.B: Prolific platform; used GenAI at work at least once per week
options.C: Prolific platform; used GenAI at work at least once per month
options.D: Internal Microsoft employees; daily GenAI users

questions.text: The paper classifies knowledge workers' critical thinking practices into three workflow phases. Which of the following is NOT one of those phases?
The paper identifies ability barriers that prevent workers from enacting critical thinking even when motivated. What are these barriers, and which did the largest number of participants report?
Across all six cognitive activities in Bloom's taxonomy, which showed the least reduction in effort when using GenAI?

The authors use the term 'stewardship' rather than 'collaboration' or 'supervision' to describe the human role in GenAI-assisted work. What reasoning do they give for preferring this term, and what does it imply about where accountability sits?

questionnaire.instructions: Please rate your experience reading this paper.

paper5.title: A Meta-Analysis of the Utility of Explainable Artificial Intelligence in Human-AI Decision-Making
paper5.taskInstructions: Use the paper to find the correct answers to the questions on the right. You may submit as many times as needed — your time and number of attempts are recorded. All multiple-choice questions must be answered correctly before you can proceed.

questions.text: After applying all inclusion criteria and filtering, how many articles were ultimately included in the meta-analysis?
The paper states two primary research comparisons it investigates. What are they, and how does the result of one change how you should interpret the other?
Which of the following was NOT listed as an inclusion criterion for studies to be included in the meta-analysis?

options.A: Studies must report mean accuracy as the performance measure
options.B: Studies must have been conducted as a between-subject design
options.C: Studies must focus on classification tasks
options.D: Studies must have been conducted in a controlled lab environment

questions.text: The subgroup analysis compared tabular and text data types. What did the results show for each, and what explanation do the authors offer for the difference?
What was the standardized mean difference (SMD) found when comparing XAI-assisted performance to human performance alone, and was it statistically significant?
The authors used a random-effects model rather than a fixed-effect model for the meta-analysis. What was the stated reason for this choice?

options.A: Random-effects models are always more accurate
options.B: The setups and populations were considerably heterogeneous between studies
options.C: The fixed-effect model is only suitable for within-subject designs
options.D: Random-effects models require fewer included studies

questions.text: The paper identifies two potential mechanisms by which XAI could improve decision-making performance. What are these two mechanisms, and which one did the qualitative review find stronger evidence for?
Which of the following was identified as a key limitation of the AI-assisted vs. XAI-assisted comparison specifically?

options.A: Too few studies reported accuracy as a metric
options.C: Small sample size due to studies not reporting dispersion of their AI-assisted condition
options.D: The tabular subgroup contained no eligible studies

questions.text: The qualitative review highlights contrasting findings across individual studies regarding how explanations affect users' acceptance of AI advice. Describe these contrasting findings and which studies reported them.
The authors raise an ethical concern in the discussion section related to one of their findings. What is this concern, and which finding prompted it?

finalQuestionnaire.instructions: Please answer these final questions about your overall experience with the highlighting system.

questions.text: Overall, how helpful were the highlighted sentences for understanding the papers?
How confident are you in your overall understanding of the three papers?
How easy was it to identify the most important information in each paper?
To what extent did the highlights include repeated or already-known information?
I felt that important information was missing from the highlighted text:
I trusted the highlights to include the key ideas of the paper:
How easy was it to tell what was new versus repeated across the papers?
The highlights helped me focus on novel or important content:
How likely would you be to use a system like this in your own reading-list workflow?
What did you like most or least about the highlighting system?
Is there anything that felt missing, confusing, or unnecessary?
